articles.title: Kerala High Court Brings Regulations To Curb Sexual Harassment Of Women At High Court
articles.summary: The Chief Justice of Kerala High Court has notified the Gender Sensitisation & Sexual Harassment of Women at the High Court of Kerala (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Regulations, 2026. The Regulations will come into effect from March 23, 2026. Definition of Sexual Harassment As per Regulation 2(m), sexual harassment includes any one or more of the following unwelcome acts or behaviour (whether directly or by implication) committed within the High Court precincts namely: physical contact

articles.title: Father Cannot Represent Son Before ITAT – No Right of Appeal: ITAT Delhi

articles.raw_summary: The commerce ministry is set to announce support measures for Indian exporters facing disruptions due to the West Asia crisis. articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court on Monday held that Article 25 of the Constitution of India protects the right to congregate for worship for every religious denomination in the country, but it does not accord protection to incitement of one faith by another in the garb of prayer. articles.title: Legal Proceedings Led To Martyrdom Of Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev And Rajguru

articles.title: NDPS Act | Prosecution Must Prove Actual Cultivation Of Cannabis, Mere Presence On Land Not Enough: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that in cases involving the alleged cultivation of cannabis under the NDPS Act, the burden lies on the prosecution to establish through cogent evidence that the accused was actually cultivating the plants, and not merely present at the spot or named in land records. articles.title: Can a Public Servant Escape Bribery Conviction Because the Co-Accused Was Acquitted? Supreme Court Clarifies Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court clarified that a public servant convicted of bribery cannot automatically escape punishment just because a co-accused was acquitted. The ruling emphasizes that the failure to prove conspiracy against one accused does not invalidate the conviction of another in bribery cases. This decision reinforces the legal framework around corruption prosecutions in India.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court explains that failure to prove conspiracy against a co-accused does not entitle a convicted public servant to acquittal in bribery cases.

articles.title: Bank-NBFC co-lending pacts may be expanded to aid MSMEs
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has recommended expanding co-lending partnerships between banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) to help micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) access cheaper credit. The committee also proposed listing profitable regional rural banks and strengthening state-run general insurers. It highlighted a credit gap for small businesses and declining insurance penetration, suggesting measures like climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rur
articles.raw_summary: A parliamentary panel suggests boosting bank and NBFC co-lending to help MSMEs access cheaper credit. It also proposes listing profitable regional rural banks and reinforcing state-run general insurers. The committee highlighted a significant credit gap for small businesses and a decline in insurance penetration. Recommendations include developing climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rural areas.

articles.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
articles.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

articles.summary: The Indian government has imposed import restrictions on silver jewellery studded with inexpensive diamonds, effective immediately until June 30, 2026. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade announced the move, shifting from unrestricted to restricted imports to curb silver imports from ASEAN nations. The policy aims to regulate imports of such jewellery.

A prolonged supply crunch will drive up food bills.

The chamber also wants the RBI to resolve issues with letters of credit. Inconsistent handling of document discrepancies by banks is a concern. Unfair practices by rating agencies are also highlighted. Clearer guidelines for banks are requested.

articles.title: India’s horticulture production to increase marginally in 2025-26: First Advance Estimate
articles.summary: India's horticulture production is set to remain steady in 2025-26. Vegetable output is expected to rise, driven by onions and potatoes. Fruit production will see a slight increase. Flower production may see a minor dip. Spice output is projected to grow significantly, with gains in cumin, ginger, garlic, and turmeric. This sector is crucial for boosting farmer incomes.

This sector is crucial for boosting farmer incomes.

articles.title: Govt approves additional 87,587 tonnes sugar export quota for 2025-26
articles.summary: In a significant move, India's food ministry has greenlit an additional quota of 87,587 tonnes of sugar exports for the upcoming 2025-26 season, responding to demands from sugar mills. These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

articles.title: DPIIT notifies changes in FDI norms for China, other land border sharing countries
articles.summary: India has taken a bold step toward enhancing its investment landscape by relaxing restrictions on foreign investments. Companies with up to ten percent Chinese shareholding can now invest seamlessly, a notable change that leaves out entities from neighboring countries, who still require prior authorization.

articles.title: Backward supply integration to trigger valuation re-rating post demerger: InGovern on UPL Global
articles.summary: Proxy Firm InGovern notes that UPL's integrated crop protection platform - UPL Global emerges as among the largest listed global crop protection pure-plays by revenue, leveraging consolidated global and Indian scale in herbicides, insecticides, fungicides, and biosolutions. It highlighted UPL Global's backend moat that assured supply from UPL Limited's Superform manufacturing, R D integration, and specialty chemicals.

Jaishankar for an important discussion on the implementation of a fresh free trade deal and a robust security partnership. This landmark agreement is set to yield significant returns for both Europe and India.

articles.title: India’s exports to US fall 13% in Feb; trade deficit with China crosses $100 billion
articles.summary: In February, India's exports to the US faced a downturn, attributed largely to the imposed American tariffs. On another front, the trade deficit with China has now exceeded 100 billion dollars for the fiscal year. Interestingly, imports from the US have ramped up considerably.

articles.title: India inks terms of reference with Philippines, Maldives for trade pact talks
articles.summary: India has initiated negotiations for trade agreements with the Philippines and the Maldives, signing terms of reference for a preferential trade agreement (PTA) with the former and a free trade agreement (FTA) with the latter. These moves come as India reviews its existing trade pacts with ASEAN and Korea, and pursues new agreements with several other nations and blocs.

articles.title: Shipping wars stitch higher costs into India’s apparel exports
articles.summary: India's apparel exporters are facing increased costs due to the West Asian conflict, with shipping routes extended and war surcharges adding ₹12 to ₹55 per garment. This disruption, including diversions around the Cape of Good Hope, delays deliveries by 10-15 days and impacts a key market for Indian ready-made garments.

This disruption, including diversions around the Cape of Good Hope, delays deliveries by 10-15 days and impacts a key market for Indian ready-made garments.

articles.title: Unemployment rate eases to 4.9% in February
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate dipped to 4.9% in February, a slight improvement from January's 5%, largely due to a significant decrease in the female jobless rate. Urban unemployment also saw a decline, while rural rates remained stable. Youth unemployment, however, experienced a marginal increase.

Urban unemployment also saw a decline, while rural rates remained stable. Youth unemployment, however, experienced a marginal increase.

articles.title: India may soften new grid-supply rules for renewable power producers
articles.summary: India might soften proposed penalties for wind and solar power companies. Developers warned stricter rules could reduce revenue and slow clean energy investment. The power regulator is now re-examining these penalties. The government aims to nearly double non-fossil fuel power capacity by 2030.

articles.title: India's February unemployment rate eases to 4.9% y/y
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate dipped to 4.9% in February, down from 5% in January, according to government data. Urban unemployment also saw a slight decrease, while rural unemployment remained steady. The overall Labour Force Participation Rate held firm, though female participation saw a marginal increase, particularly in rural areas.

The overall Labour Force Participation Rate held firm, though female participation saw a marginal increase, particularly in rural areas.

articles.title: CCI plans to check abuse in AI space
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has issued a guidance note urging stakeholders such as boards, management teams, and decision-makers to conduct self-audits of their AI systems. The goal is to ensure that AI applications do not have hidden anti-competitive outcomes during development, deployment, and monitoring. This move aims to proactively address potential competition issues in the rapidly evolving AI space in India.
articles.raw_summary: As an initial step, the watchdog has issued a guidance note encouraging stakeholders—including boards, management teams and decision-makers—to conduct selfaudits of their AI systems to ensure that “at the stage of development, deployment and monitoring of those Al applications there aren't any hidden anti-competitive outcomes".

articles.title: India needs outcome-driven urban policy, says EAC-PM paper
articles.summary: The paper titled ‘India’s Hidden Urbanisation and its Policy Implications’ written by EAC-PM member Shamika Ravi and its consultants Manuj Joshi and Apurv Kumar Mishra suggests disbursement of funds under Union schemes over the next five years to be linked to needs on the ground along with financing, governance and land reforms in the sector.

articles.title: Iran war begins to bomb the GST bonanza. What can happen?
articles.summary: Iran War: Escalating geopolitical tensions in West Asia are threatening to reverse gains from India's GST rationalisation. Rising crude-linked input costs, supply chain disruptions, and higher freight rates are forcing companies to consider price hikes, potentially eroding demand momentum. While the broader consumption outlook remains resilient, inflation risks are increasing.

articles.title: India's sugar production up 10.4% so far this season
articles.summary: From October to March, Indian sugar mills have achieved a colossal production of over 26 million metric tons of sugar, showcasing a notable increase of 10.4% compared to the previous year. The rise in output can be largely attributed to the robust performance of Maharashtra and Karnataka, complemented by a slight boost from Uttar Pradesh.

articles.title: India's trade deficit narrows on month to $27.1 billion in Feb; Tariff uncertainty, Iran conflict loom
articles.summary: India's merchandise trade deficit widened to $27.1 billion in February 2026, driven by increased imports. While overall exports saw a slight rise year-on-year, rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia and tariff disputes are beginning to impact trade flows and exporter sentiment, with potential for further widening of the deficit.

articles.title: Wholesale inflation at 11-month high of 2.13%
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation reached a new high in February. Prices for food and other goods increased significantly. This trend mirrors rising retail inflation. Experts predict further price hikes ahead. Deflation continues in fuel and power. Food inflation saw a notable rise. Manufacturing prices also edged up.

articles.title: India’s basmati rice exports to stay steady despite Middle East conflict: Crisil
articles.summary: Indian basmati rice exports are projected to stay stable this fiscal and next. Growth of up to two percent is anticipated over last year's volume. While exports to Iran may dip, increased demand from Saudi Arabia, Iraq, UAE, and Yemen will compensate. Logistical issues might stretch working capital, but exporters can pass on costs.

articles.title: India’s wholesale inflation hits 11-month high of 2.13% in February
articles.summary: India's wholesale price inflation saw an increase in February. The rate rose to 2.13 percent from 1.81 percent in January. This rise was influenced by higher prices in manufacturing, metals, food, and textiles. Food inflation also climbed to 1.85 percent. These changes indicate growing cost pressures across key economic sectors.

These changes indicate growing cost pressures across key economic sectors.

articles.title: Waaree Energies shares rocket 12%, up 18% in 3 sessions! What’s behind the stellar rally?
articles.summary: ​ Waaree Energies shares climbed significantly on Wednesday. This surge follows the company's announcement of a major solar ingot and wafer manufacturing facility in Nagpur. The 10 GW capacity unit is set to boost India's clean energy manufacturing. Additionally, Waaree Energies is now the title sponsor for Rajasthan Royals. This partnership aims to promote clean energy as a people's movement.

articles.summary: The US Federal Reserve is expected to maintain interest rates at 3.5%-3.75% during its March 17-18 meeting due to rising inflation, labor market concerns, and geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the US. The decision may influence India's economic policies, particularly in trade and energy sectors, given its reliance on oil imports and trade relations with the US.

articles.title: Memory chip shortage to continue until 2030, says SK Group chairman
articles.summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won predicts a global shortage of memory chips will persist for the next 4-5 years, with supply unable to meet demand until at least 2030. The shortage is driven by dominant players like SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron struggling to keep up with demand. This could impact industries reliant on semiconductors, including India's growing electronics and tech sectors. The situation may prompt policy measures to address supply chain vulnerabilities or boost domestic production
articles.raw_summary: Chey Tae-won, SK Group chairman, predicts a global memory chip shortage for 4-5 more years. SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron control supply but can't meet demand until 2030.

articles.title: 47% of sitting MLAs in poll-bound West Bengal face criminal cases: ADR
articles.summary: A report by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) reveals that 47% of sitting MLAs in West Bengal face criminal cases, with 37% facing serious charges. The findings come ahead of the state's elections on April 23 and 29, with results declared on May 4. The report also notes that 52% of MLAs are crorepatis and 15% are women. The data raises questions about governance and electoral integrity in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Nearly half of West Bengal's legislators have criminal cases, says ADR. Elections are on April 23 and 29, results on May 4. 47% face charges, 37% serious. 52% are crorepatis, 15% women.

articles.title: NCP (SP) won't contest against Sunetra Pawar in Baramati assembly bypoll: Sule
articles.summary: NCP (SP) working president Supriya Sule announced that her party will not contest the upcoming Baramati assembly bypoll against Deputy Chief Minister and NCP leader Sunetra Pawar. This decision reflects internal party dynamics and electoral strategy in the bypoll, which is a local-level election to fill a vacant assembly seat.

articles.title: Rajya Sabha Election 2026: What is cross-voting
articles.summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.
articles.raw_summary: Cross-voting in Rajya Sabha elections refers to when a Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) votes for a candidate from a different party, contrary to their own party's directive or candidate preferences.

articles.title: Assembly elections 2026: EC imposes Model Code of Conduct; what's not allowed in poll-bound states
articles.summary: The Election Commission has imposed the Model Code of Conduct for the upcoming assembly elections in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry. This restricts certain activities in these poll-bound states to ensure fair elections. The code is a regulatory measure that impacts political campaigns and governance during the election period.
articles.raw_summary: Assembly elections will be held in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Assam, along with the Union Territory of Puducherry.

The outcome could impact Tamil Nadu's political landscape ahead of elections.
